rationale: CIS 1.1 mandates a detailed enterprise asset inventory, which implements the asset inventory aspect of ISO A.5.9 but the ISO control additionally covers software and associated assets more broadly.
CIS 1.2 addresses unauthorized asset remediation, covering a reactive enforcement sub-element of the broader ISO A.5.9 asset inventory requirement.
CIS 1.3 specifies active discovery tooling to maintain asset inventory, implementing a technical sub-requirement of ISO A.5.9.
CIS 1.4 uses DHCP logging to update asset records, providing a specific technical mechanism contributing to ISO A.5.9 completeness.
CIS 1.5 requires passive asset discovery tooling, supplementing the asset inventory maintenance goal of ISO A.5.9.
CIS 2.1 mandates a software inventory, implementing the software asset identification sub-requirement within ISO A.5.9.
CIS 2.2 requires only supported software to be deployed, addressing a currency/lifecycle dimension of software asset management under ISO A.5.9.
CIS 2.3 addresses unauthorized software, implementing enforcement aspects of ISO A.5.9 software inventory and A.8.19 installation controls.
CIS 2.3 unauthorized software remediation partially overlaps with ISO A.8.19 secure installation of software on operational systems.
CIS 2.4 automates software inventory tooling, implementing a technical delivery mechanism for ISO A.5.9 software asset inventory.
CIS 2.5 application allowlisting restricts software installation to authorized items, implementing a specific technical control within ISO A.8.19.
CIS 2.6 library allowlisting constrains which libraries may execute, narrowing the ISO A.8.19 software installation control to library-level.
CIS 2.7 script allowlisting restricts execution to authorized scripts, targeting script-level enforcement within ISO A.8.19.
CIS 3.1 establishes a data management process, implementing an operational process aligned with the ISO A.5.12 information classification policy requirement.
CIS 3.1 data management process also partially addresses acceptable use of information covered by ISO A.5.10.
CIS 3.2 establishes a data inventory, providing the asset enumeration prerequisite for ISO A.5.12 classification.
CIS 3.2 data inventory overlaps with ISO A.5.9 which covers inventory of information assets.
CIS 3.3 configures data access control lists, implementing technical enforcement of access rules per ISO A.5.15 access control.
CIS 3.3 ACL configuration partially overlaps with ISO A.8.3 information access restriction.
CIS 3.4 enforces data retention schedules, implementing a technical sub-requirement of the ISO A.5.33 protection of records control.
CIS 3.5 requires secure data disposal procedures, implementing a sub-element of ISO A.7.14 secure disposal or reuse of equipment.
CIS 3.5 data deletion also overlaps with ISO A.8.10 information deletion which covers secure deletion from systems.
CIS 3.6 encrypts end-user devices, implementing a specific cryptographic measure within the broader ISO A.8.24 use of cryptography control.
CIS 3.7 establishes and maintains a data classification scheme, directly equivalent to the ISO A.5.12 information classification requirement.
CIS 3.8 documents data flows, addressing the data transfer identification sub-element of ISO A.5.14 information transfer control.
CIS 3.9 encrypts data on removable media, implementing a specific physical/removable-media cryptographic measure within ISO A.8.24.
CIS 3.10 encrypts sensitive data in transit, targeting the in-transit encryption sub-requirement of ISO A.8.24.
CIS 3.11 encrypts sensitive data at rest, targeting the at-rest encryption sub-requirement of ISO A.8.24.
CIS 3.12 segments data processing and storage by sensitivity, implementing network/data-level segregation as addressed in ISO A.8.22 segregation of networks.
CIS 3.13 deploys a data loss prevention solution, directly equivalent to the ISO A.8.12 data leakage prevention control.
CIS 3.14 logs sensitive data access, implementing a focused logging sub-requirement within ISO A.8.15 logging.
CIS 4.1 establishes a secure configuration process, implementing the baseline process aspect of ISO A.8.9 configuration management.
CIS 4.2 extends the secure configuration process specifically to network infrastructure, a sub-domain of ISO A.8.9.
CIS 4.3 configures automatic session locking, implementing a specific user endpoint security measure within ISO A.8.1 user endpoint devices.
CIS 4.4 manages server-side firewalls, implementing a specific firewall measure contributing to ISO A.8.20 networks security.
CIS 4.5 manages firewalls on end-user devices, extending ISO A.8.20 network security controls to endpoint layer.
CIS 4.6 securely manages enterprise assets and software configurations, addressing an ongoing maintenance sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9.
CIS 4.7 manages and removes default accounts on devices and software, implementing a specific hardening element of ISO A.8.9 configuration management.
CIS 4.8 disables unnecessary services, implementing a service hardening sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9.
CIS 4.9 enforces trusted DNS server configuration, implementing a network service hardening sub-element of ISO A.8.20.
CIS 4.10 enforces automatic lockout on portable devices, implementing a specific physical/logical security sub-requirement of ISO A.8.1.
CIS 4.11 requires remote wipe capability on portable devices, implementing a mobile device management sub-requirement of ISO A.8.1.
CIS 4.12 separates enterprise workspaces on mobile devices, implementing a containerization sub-requirement of ISO A.8.1 for BYOD scenarios.
CIS 5.1 establishes an account inventory, implementing the user identity enumeration sub-requirement of ISO A.5.16 identity management.
CIS 5.2 requires unique passwords, implementing the password uniqueness sub-requirement of ISO A.5.17 authentication information.
CIS 5.3 disables dormant accounts, implementing the access revocation for inactive accounts sub-requirement of ISO A.5.18 access rights.
CIS 5.4 restricts admin privileges to dedicated admin accounts, directly equivalent to ISO A.8.2 privileged access rights management.
CIS 5.5 maintains a service account inventory, addressing the non-human identity sub-requirement within ISO A.5.16 identity management.
CIS 5.6 centralizes account management, implementing a centralized identity governance sub-requirement of ISO A.5.16.
CIS 6.1 establishes a formal access granting process, implementing the access provisioning process sub-requirement of ISO A.5.18.
CIS 6.2 establishes a formal access revoking process, implementing the access revocation sub-requirement of ISO A.5.18.
CIS 6.3 requires MFA for externally-exposed applications, implementing a specific strong authentication sub-requirement of ISO A.8.5 secure authentication.
CIS 6.4 requires MFA for remote network access, implementing a remote-access-specific strong authentication sub-requirement of ISO A.8.5.
CIS 6.5 requires MFA for administrative access, implementing a privileged-access strong authentication sub-requirement of ISO A.8.5.
CIS 6.6 maintains an inventory of authentication and authorization systems, addressing a specific asset enumeration sub-requirement within ISO A.5.16.
CIS 6.7 centralizes access control, implementing the centralized enforcement sub-requirement of ISO A.5.15 access control policy.
CIS 6.8 defines and maintains role-based access control, implementing the RBAC sub-requirement of ISO A.5.15 access control.
CIS 7.1 establishes a vulnerability management process, implementing the procedural framework sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
CIS 7.2 establishes a remediation process for vulnerabilities, implementing the remediation workflow sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 7.3 performs automated OS patch management, implementing the OS patch sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 7.4 performs automated application patch management, implementing the application patch sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 7.5 performs automated vulnerability scans of internal assets, implementing an internal scan sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 7.6 performs automated vulnerability scans of external assets, implementing an externally-exposed scan sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 7.7 remediates detected vulnerabilities within defined timeframes, implementing the remediation execution sub-requirement of ISO A.8.8.
CIS 8.1 establishes an audit log management process, implementing the procedural framework sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15 logging.
CIS 8.2 collects audit logs from enterprise assets, implementing the log collection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.3 ensures adequate storage for audit logs, implementing the log storage capacity sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.4 standardizes time synchronization across enterprise assets, directly equivalent to ISO A.8.17 clock synchronization.
CIS 8.5 collects detailed audit logs including user and process information, implementing a log enrichment sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.6 collects DNS query audit logs, implementing a specific DNS-level log collection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.7 collects URL request audit logs, implementing a specific web-layer log collection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.8 collects command-line audit logs, implementing a specific process/CLI log collection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.9 centralizes audit logs in a SIEM or log aggregator, implementing the centralized log management sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.10 defines and enforces audit log retention periods, implementing the retention sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15.
CIS 8.11 conducts regular audit log reviews, implementing the monitoring activities sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16 monitoring activities.
CIS 8.12 collects service provider logs for third-party oversight, implementing a supplier-log sub-requirement within ISO A.8.15.
CIS 9.1 ensures only supported browsers and email clients are used, implementing a software currency sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9 configuration management.
CIS 9.2 uses DNS filtering to block malicious domains, implementing a network-level filtering sub-requirement of ISO A.8.22 segregation of networks.
CIS 9.3 enforces network-based URL filtering, implementing a URL-level network filtering sub-requirement of ISO A.8.22.
CIS 9.4 restricts unauthorized browser extensions, implementing a configuration hardening sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9.
CIS 9.5 implements DMARC for email authentication, implementing a specific email protection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.23 web filtering.
CIS 9.6 blocks unnecessary file types in email/web, implementing a content filtering sub-requirement of ISO A.8.23.
CIS 9.7 deploys anti-malware protection on email servers, implementing a mail-server-specific sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7 protection against malware.
CIS 10.1 deploys and maintains anti-malware software, implementing the anti-malware deployment sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.2 configures automatic anti-malware signature updates, implementing the signature currency sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.3 disables autorun for removable media, implementing a specific malware-entry-point control within ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.4 configures automatic malware scanning of removable media, implementing a removable-media scan sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.5 enables anti-exploitation features such as DEP and ASLR, implementing an exploit-mitigation sub-requirement within ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.6 centrally manages anti-malware software, implementing the centralized management sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7.
CIS 10.7 deploys behavior-based anti-malware, implementing an advanced threat detection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.7.
CIS 11.1 establishes a data recovery process, implementing the backup and recovery process sub-requirement of ISO A.8.13 information backup.
CIS 11.2 performs automated backups of in-scope data, directly equivalent to the ISO A.8.13 information backup requirement.
CIS 11.3 protects recovery data integrity and confidentiality, implementing the backup protection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.13.
CIS 11.4 establishes an isolated backup instance to prevent ransomware impact, implementing the backup isolation sub-requirement of ISO A.8.13.
CIS 11.5 tests data recovery to verify backup integrity, implementing the backup testing sub-requirement of ISO A.8.13.
CIS 12.1 keeps network infrastructure current and patched, implementing the network infrastructure maintenance sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9.
CIS 12.2 establishes a secure network architecture, implementing the architectural design sub-requirement of ISO A.8.20 networks security.
CIS 12.3 securely manages network infrastructure including change controls, implementing the management sub-requirement of ISO A.8.20.
CIS 12.4 requires and maintains up-to-date network architecture diagrams, implementing the documentation sub-requirement within ISO A.8.20.
CIS 12.5 centralizes network AAA, implementing a centralized authentication enforcement sub-requirement of ISO A.8.20.
CIS 12.6 requires use of secure management protocols (e.g. SSH, SNMPv3), implementing a secure protocol sub-requirement of ISO A.8.20.
CIS 12.7 requires remote devices to use VPN and connect to enterprise AAA, implementing a remote access security sub-requirement of ISO A.8.20.
CIS 12.8 establishes dedicated computing resources for administrative work, implementing the separation of development/test/production sub-requirement of ISO A.8.31.
CIS 13.1 centralizes security event alerting, implementing the alerting sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16 monitoring activities.
CIS 13.2 deploys host-based intrusion detection, implementing a host-level detection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16.
CIS 13.3 deploys network intrusion detection, implementing a network-level detection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16.
CIS 13.4 performs traffic filtering between network segments, implementing inter-segment filtering within ISO A.8.22 segregation of networks.
CIS 13.5 manages access control for remote assets, implementing a remote-access-specific enforcement sub-requirement of ISO A.5.15.
CIS 13.6 collects network traffic flow logs, implementing a network-layer log collection sub-requirement of ISO A.8.15 logging.
CIS 13.7 deploys host-based intrusion prevention, implementing a host-level prevention sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16.
CIS 13.8 deploys network intrusion prevention, implementing a network-level prevention sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16.
CIS 13.9 deploys port-level access control such as 802.1x, implementing a network port security sub-requirement within ISO A.8.20.
CIS 13.10 performs application-layer filtering, implementing a deep packet inspection sub-requirement within ISO A.8.22.
CIS 13.11 tunes security event alerting thresholds to reduce noise, implementing the monitoring optimization sub-requirement of ISO A.8.16.
CIS 14.1 establishes and maintains a security awareness program, directly equivalent to ISO A.6.3 information security awareness, education and training.
CIS 14.2 provides targeted training on recognizing social engineering attacks, implementing a specific threat-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.3 trains on authentication best practices, implementing an authentication-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.4 trains on data handling best practices, implementing a data protection awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.5 trains on causes of unintentional data exposure, implementing a data-loss-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.6 trains on recognizing and reporting incidents, implementing an incident-reporting-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.7 trains on identifying missing security updates, implementing a patch-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.8 trains on insecure network dangers, implementing a network-security-awareness sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 14.9 conducts role-specific security training, implementing a targeted/role-based training sub-requirement of ISO A.6.3.
CIS 15.1 establishes a service provider inventory, implementing the supplier identification sub-requirement of ISO A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
CIS 15.2 establishes a service provider management policy, implementing the policy framework sub-requirement of ISO A.5.19.
CIS 15.3 classifies service providers by risk level, implementing a risk-tiering sub-requirement within ISO A.5.19.
CIS 15.4 ensures service provider contracts include security requirements, directly equivalent to ISO A.5.20 addressing information security within supplier agreements.
CIS 15.5 assesses service providers for security compliance, implementing the monitoring and review sub-requirement of ISO A.5.22 monitoring and review of supplier services.
CIS 15.6 monitors service providers for ongoing security posture, implementing the ongoing monitoring sub-requirement of ISO A.5.22.
CIS 15.7 covers secure decommissioning of service providers, implementing an offboarding sub-requirement within ISO A.5.19 supplier relationship management.
CIS 16.1 establishes a secure application development process, implementing the secure development lifecycle sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25 secure development life cycle.
CIS 16.2 establishes a process to accept and address software vulnerabilities, implementing the vulnerability handling sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.3 performs root cause analysis on security vulnerabilities, implementing a quality improvement sub-requirement within ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.4 maintains an inventory of third-party software components, implementing a software supply chain inventory sub-requirement within ISO A.5.9.
CIS 16.4 third-party component inventory also partially addresses ISO A.5.21 managing information security in the ICT supply chain.
CIS 16.5 requires use of current and trusted third-party software components, implementing a dependency management sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.6 establishes a severity rating system for application vulnerabilities, implementing a risk-scoring sub-requirement within ISO A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities.
CIS 16.7 uses standard hardening templates for application infrastructure, implementing an application-hardening sub-requirement of ISO A.8.9 configuration management.
CIS 16.8 requires separation of production and non-production environments, directly equivalent to ISO A.8.31 separation of development, test and production environments.
CIS 16.9 trains developers in application security and secure coding, implementing a developer-training sub-requirement within ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.10 applies secure design principles in application architectures, implementing a security-by-design sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.11 uses vetted modules for authentication and cryptography, implementing a reuse-of-approved-components sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25.
CIS 16.12 implements code-level security checks, implementing a security testing in development sub-requirement of ISO A.8.29 security testing in development and acceptance.
CIS 16.13 conducts application penetration testing, implementing a formal security testing sub-requirement of ISO A.8.29.
CIS 16.14 conducts threat modeling in design, implementing a design-time threat analysis sub-requirement of ISO A.8.25 secure development lifecycle.
CIS 17.1 designates personnel to manage incident handling, implementing the roles and responsibilities sub-requirement of ISO A.5.24 information security incident management planning.
CIS 17.2 maintains contact information for reporting incidents, implementing the reporting channel sub-requirement of ISO A.5.24.
CIS 17.3 establishes an enterprise process for reporting security incidents, implementing an employee reporting mechanism sub-requirement of ISO A.6.8 information security event reporting.
CIS 17.4 establishes and maintains an incident response process, directly equivalent to ISO A.5.26 response to information security incidents.
CIS 17.5 assigns key incident response roles and responsibilities, implementing the staffing sub-requirement of ISO A.5.24 incident management planning.
CIS 17.6 defines communication mechanisms for incident response, implementing the communications sub-requirement of ISO A.5.24.
CIS 17.7 conducts routine incident response exercises, implementing the testing and exercises sub-requirement of ISO A.5.24.
CIS 17.8 conducts post-incident reviews to extract lessons learned, directly equivalent to ISO A.5.27 learning from information security incidents.
CIS 17.9 establishes incident severity thresholds, implementing the incident assessment and categorization sub-requirement of ISO A.5.25 assessment and decision on information security events.

CIS 18.1 establishes and maintains a penetration testing program, implementing a technical verification sub-requirement within ISO A.5.36 compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security.
CIS 18.1 penetration testing program also partially overlaps with ISO A.8.8 vulnerability management as a validation mechanism.
CIS 18.2 performs periodic external penetration tests, implementing an external attack-surface verification sub-requirement of ISO A.5.36.
CIS 18.3 remediates penetration test findings within defined timeframes, implementing a finding-remediation sub-requirement of ISO A.5.36.
CIS 18.4 validates security measures against defined benchmarks after remediation, implementing a security validation sub-requirement of ISO A.5.36.
CIS 18.5 performs periodic internal penetration tests, implementing an internal attack-surface verification sub-requirement of ISO A.5.36.
